summary: Implemented a bounded deterministic M6 layered AI planner and validation runner in sim-ai. The planner uses an explicit Alpha agenda with strategic, campaign, recovery, succession, diplomatic, and policy layers; emits shared GameCommand payloads through submitCommandV1; keeps non-system gameplay actions on AI actors; preserves existing system-only support commands for scheduler/legitimacy authority; and exposes bounded reason traces for no-action, wait, withdraw, target, change, policy, and diplomacy decisions.

tests_run.result: Before implementation commit: modified packages/sim-ai/package.json and packages/sim-ai/src/index.ts; untracked packages/sim-ai/src/m6-layered-ai.ts and tests/sim-ai-m6-layered-ai.test.ts. All paths were allowed by the task.
Pre-implementation regression failed as expected because runM6LayeredAlphaAiValidationV1 and planM6LayeredAiTurnV1 were not implemented.

Post-implementation passed 3 test files / 6 tests, including full Alpha survival, command parity, bounded trace truncation, deterministic plan equality, no pre-submit mutation, no rescue hooks, and reason coverage.
Passed 25 test files / 191 tests. Node emitted the existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning for tests/fixtures/deterministic-rng-worker.ts.
Passed; hello, command/query, save/load, M4, M5, and M6 Alpha Node/Worker-compatible hashes matched. M6 Alpha Node hash 260f46c3 matched Worker-compatible hash 260f46c3; terminal outcome victory.
Passed architecture:check, sim:determinism, root vitest 40 files / 304 tests, and apps/web vitest 1 file / 4 tests.
Task JSON valid before REVIEW metadata update.
No whitespace errors before REVIEW metadata update.

decisions: Kept the implementation in packages/sim-ai and tests; no protocol, save-format, scheduler, RNG, UI, content, runner, or docs changes were made.
Used an explicit bounded Alpha agenda instead of a future-proof manager, singleton scorer, script engine, telemetry, service, or manual battle assumption.

Converted player-available gameplay command templates to AI actors at planning time while preserving system-only scheduler and M6 legitimacy support commands because sim-core currently authorizes those only for domain system actors.
The layered planner reads public query surfaces for succession, diplomacy, and terminal preview reasons where available; it does not mutate WorldState before submitCommandV1.
risks: Task risk remains R4 and needs systems_architect review. No new R3 protocol/save/scheduler/RNG change was introduced by this writer.

Policy active-event selection still relies on the accepted M6 Alpha fixture agenda because no public policy-event list query exists yet; the action is still submitted through sim.choose-policy-event-option and covered by command parity tests.
Postwar legitimacy remains a system support command under existing sim-core authority rules, so it is tracked separately from non-system AI gameplay actions.
requested_action: Review the bounded layered AI implementation and tests for deterministic ordering, command parity, trace coverage, authority boundaries, and whether the system-support handling for scheduler/postwar legitimacy is acceptable for M6 Alpha. If accepted, route onward per the M6 review flow; do not mark ACCEPTED/CLOSED from this writer handoff alone.
